title: Lesson 49 — Sound and Silence

words.definition: loud and harsh; also, presenting a point of view in an excessively forceful way
words.example: A strident voice cut through the noisy hall.
A shrill whistle signaled the start of practice.

words.definition: deep, clear, and continuing to sound or reverberate
words.example: His resonant baritone filled the small chapel.

words.definition: having a full, deep, or rich sound
words.example: The organ's sonorous notes rolled through the cathedral.

words.definition: made quieter or less clear, as if by a covering
words.example: A muffled voice came from behind the thick door.

words.definition: quiet and still; subdued in sound
words.example: A hushed silence fell as the verdict was read.
The gunshot reverberated across the canyon.

words.definition: refraining from speech or temporarily speechless; silent
words.example: He stood mute, unable to answer the accusation.
Her reply was nearly inaudible over the engine noise.

words.definition: a loud and confused noise, especially of shouting
words.example: A clamor rose from the crowd as the gates opened.

words.definition: a loud, unpleasant, and prolonged noise
words.example: You could barely talk over the din of the factory floor.

words.definition: vehement or loud in expressing one's opinions
words.example: A vociferous minority dominated the public comment period.

story: The concert hall was famous for its acoustics, though not everyone in the audience noticed. A [[strident|strident]] cough, [[shrill|shrill]] and sudden, briefly broke the mood before the soprano's [[resonant|resonant]], [[sonorous|sonorous]] aria began. Even the [[muffled|muffled]] sound of latecomers settling into seats gave way to a [[hushed|hushed]] stillness as her final note began to [[reverberate|reverberate]] through the balcony. For one long moment the whole room went nearly [[mute|mute]], the shuffle of programs [[inaudible|inaudible]]. Then came the [[clamor|clamor]] — a joyful [[din|din]] of applause, and one [[vociferous|vociferous]] fan shouting for an encore before the lights had even come up.
